chapter_title.en: Chapter 80 – Goodness in the vicinity of the Bounties

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/167)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/167)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ أَحْسِنُوا جِوَارَ النِّعَمِ قُلْتُ وَ مَا حُسْنُ جِوَارِ النِّعَمِ قَالَ الشُّكْرُ لِمَنْ أَنْعَمَ بِهَا وَ أَدَاءُ حُقُوقِهَا .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Abu Abdullah, from Usman Bin Isa, from Muhammad Bin Ijlan who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘Be good in the vicinity of the Bounty’. I said, ‘And what is being good in the vicinity of the Bounty?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The gratefulness to the one who has favoured (you) with it, and fulfilments of its rights’.
en.sarwar: 2. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn abu ‘Abd Allah from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from Muhammad ibn ‘Ajlan who has said the following: “I heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, say, ‘Keep good neighborly relations with bounties/ I then asked, ‘How is that done?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘Thank the One who has granted bounties and pay the rights due on the bounties/”
